info.title: Airbyte Agent API
info.summary: Airbyte's Agent API retrieves data on behalf of your workspaces and feeds it to your agentic workflows.
info.description:
This is the public API documentation for Airbyte's Agent API. Use the API to manage authentication, connectors, and workspaces, and power their agentic workflows.

If you are a member of multiple Airbyte Agents organizations, add the `X-Organization-Id: organization's UUID` header to your requests. It specifies which organization you are making requests to. If you only belong to one organization, you don't need to set this header.

The API contains several types of endpoints.

- Authentication: Generate tokens to make other API requests.
- Connectors: Create and manage end-user connectors, and execute operations with connectors.
- Workspaces: manage workspaces and retrieve workspace statistics.
- Data replication: configure connection templates and destination definitions for data replication use cases.

Some endpoints are separated into Advanced categories. These endpoints are more specialized and should not be required for day-to-day use of the API.

delete.description:
Soft-delete a test organization by ID.

Used by E2E monitoring tests to clean up test organizations after test completion.
Sets tombstone=True on the organization record using the IsDeletable mixin.

When cancel_subscriptions is True (default), also cancels:
- Orb subscription (for billing cleanup)
- Stigg subscription (for entitlement cleanup)

Safety features:
- Requires admin authentication via authorized_instance_admin dependency
- Returns 404 if organization doesn't exist (including already-deleted orgs)
- Supports dry_run mode for testing
- Uses admin database session to bypass RLS
- Subscription cancellation failures are logged but don't block deletion
- Idempotent: attempting to delete an already-deleted org returns 404

Args:
    organization_id: UUID of the organization to soft-delete
    dry_run: If True, validate but don't commit the deletion
    cancel_subscriptions: If True, cancel Orb and Stigg subscriptions
    admin_db: Admin database session (bypasses RLS)
    secret_store: Secret store for repository initialization

Raises:
    HTTPException 404: Organization not found (including already-deleted orgs)
    HTTPException 401: Not authenticated as instance admin

delete.description:
Hard-delete an E2E test organization and all related resources.

⚠️  WARNING: DESTRUCTIVE OPERATION - USE ONLY FOR E2E TEST CLEANUP ⚠️

This endpoint performs IRREVERSIBLE hard-deletion of organizations and ALL
related resources. It is specifically designed ONLY for cleaning up organizations
created during automated Devin E2E onboarding tests.

DO NOT use this endpoint for:
- Production organizations
- Customer organizations
- Existing test organizations used across multiple test suites

The GitHub workflow is gated to only call this for onboarding tests where
a NEW organization is created (matrix.test.test_type == 'onboarding').

This endpoint performs comprehensive hard-deletion of:
- Sonar DB: Organization + ConnectorOAuthParameter records
- Sonar AWS: Secrets for OAuth parameters and organization
- Airbyte DB: Secret storage configurations (organization-scoped)
- Airbyte DB: Actor OAuth parameters (organization-scoped)
- Airbyte: Workspaces (all workspaces for the organization)
- External: Orb subscriptions (cancelled immediately)
- External: Stigg subscriptions (cancelled immediately)

Safety features:
- Requires admin authentication via authorized_instance_admin dependency
- Validates organization email matches E2E test pattern (sonar.production.testuser)
- Uses admin database session to bypass RLS
- Graceful error handling (external service failures don't block DB cleanup)
- Returns cleanup counts for observability

Args:
    organization_id: UUID of the organization to hard-delete
    admin_db: Admin database session (bypasses RLS)
    secret_store: Secret store for AWS secret deletion

Returns:
    dict: Cleanup counts showing resources deleted

Raises:
    HTTPException 404: Organization not found
    HTTPException 403: Organization email does not match E2E test pattern
    HTTPException 401: Not authenticated as instance admin
    HTTPException 500: Cleanup failed (check logs for details)

parameters.name: organization_id

schema.description: Organization ID to hard-delete
schema.title: Organization Id

parameters.description: Organization ID to hard-delete

ConnectorExecuteRequest.title: ConnectorExecuteRequest
ConnectorExecuteRequest.description:
Request body for connector execution.

All actions use the same unified format:
    - entity: resource type or stream name
    - action: operation type (list, get, create, download, search, api_search)
    - params: operation-specific parameters

For SEARCH action, params should contain:
    - query: Query object with filter/sort conditions
    - limit: maximum number of results (default 10)
    - cursor: pagination cursor for next page
    - fields: list of field paths to include in results

Encryption mapper - encrypts sensitive fields with RSA.

Encrypts specified fields using RSA public key encryption. The encrypted
values replace the original field values in the output stream.

Note: Only RSA encryption is supported. AES is not available due to
secrets hydration issues in the Airbyte platform.

public_key.title: Public Key
public_key.description: RSA public key in PEM format

EncryptionMapperConfig.title: EncryptionMapperConfig
EncryptionMapperConfig.description:
Configuration for encryption mapper - uses snake_case.

Encrypts a specified field using RSA (OAEP-SHA256). The encrypted value
replaces the original field value or is emitted with the configured suffix.

NOTE: Symmetric AES is NOT supported in this API.

Why we don't support AES:
- Secrets hydration for AES mappers is not currently supported in the platform
- AES requires symmetric key management which doesn't align well with
  Sonar's secrets management architecture
- RSA with public keys is more appropriate for this use case as it
  allows encryption without exposing private keys in the configuration

If you need AES encryption, this must be resolved at the platform level
with proper secrets storage and hydration support before we can add it here.

EnrollmentStatusResponse.title: EnrollmentStatusResponse
EnrollmentStatusResponse.description:
Response schema for enrollment status endpoint.

Used to determine if a user is new vs existing, and whether
their organization is enrolled in Sonar.

For existing Airbyte Cloud users who have enrolled, the `organization_id`
will be their embedded org ID (not their cloud org ID), and
`embedded_organization_id` will also be set for clarity.

NOTE: `error_message` is intentionally NOT included here. Error messages
are stored in the DB (for admin debugging) but not exposed to clients
to prevent leaking internal details.

SecretMigrationStatus.title: SecretMigrationStatus
SecretMigrationStatus.description:
Status of secret storage migration during organization enrollment.

- NOT_STARTED: Migration has not been initiated (should not appear in practice)
- IN_PROGRESS: Migration is currently running
- COMPLETED: Secrets successfully migrated from default to organization-specific storage
- FAILED: Migration encountered an error (enrollment continues, status tracked for monitoring)
- NOT_APPLICABLE: No migration needed (CUSTOMER_OWNED storage, or no default secrets to migrate)
